project.name: audio-layer
project.description: Record without a meeting bot. Leave with a transcript, live summary, key points, action items, follow-up context, and searchable memory your AI tools can use.

inputs.customer: Founders, product teams, and GTM teams that run frequent planning, customer, and sales meetings and need decisions, owners, follow-ups, and context available immediately after the call.
inputs.problem: Important decisions and action items get scattered across transcripts, manual notes, Slack, email, calendars, and AI chats. Existing meeting bots can feel intrusive, miss context, and rarely produce structured outputs that teams can search, share, or reuse in AI tools.
inputs.currentWorkaround: Manual notes, Zoom or Meet transcripts, Otter or Fathom style meeting bots, Notion or Docs, Tana style knowledge graphs, Slack recaps, and ad hoc copy-paste into ChatGPT or Claude.
inputs.proposedSolution: Record without a meeting bot. Leave with a transcript, live summary, key points, action items, follow-up context, and searchable memory your AI tools can use.
inputs.pricing: Free $0, Core $20 per user per month, Pro $30 per user per month. Pricing must stay easy to change before launch.
inputs.distribution: Download-first launch from the Layer One website with platform-aware Mac and Windows links and mobile app-store links as builds become available. Pair with founder-led outreach to product teams, AI productivity users, and early customer conversations.
inputs.timing: Teams are adopting AI tools faster than their meeting context can move with them. Better speech models, cheaper summarization, and desktop or mobile distribution make a private meeting memory layer practical now.
inputs.constraints: Do not commit secrets. Keep provider keys in env. Ship AssemblyAI today, add Deepgram Live when the API key is supplied, keep Stripe Free/Core/Pro configurable, and verify primary routes with browser proof.

bestCustomer: Founders, product teams, and GTM teams that run frequent planning, customer, and sales meetings and need decisions, owners, follow-ups, and context available immediately after the call.
mvpScope: One primary workflow that proves the painful job can be completed.
One measurable activation moment and one retention reason.
One priced offer or concierge/manual validation path before broad automation.
Repo evidence: plan, tests, browser proof, docs, scorecard, and handoff report.
pricingRecommendation: Free $0, Core $20 per user per month, Pro $30 per user per month. Pricing must stay easy to change before launch.
gtmPlan: Download-first launch from the Layer One website with platform-aware Mac and Windows links and mobile app-store links as builds become available. Pair with founder-led outreach to product teams, AI productivity users, and early customer conversations.
Write the landing-page promise from the customer/problem/current-workaround answers.
Run 5-10 direct customer conversations before treating the scope as validated.

dataAndApiRisks: AI Gateway model availability and spend must be observed in telemetry.
securityRisks: Do not commit secrets; setup writes .env.example only.
Validate inputs and tool schemas for every API/tool surface.
Record failure modes for paid or user-data integrations.
reliabilityNeeds: Fallback shapes for dashboards/APIs when optional services are absent.
Contract tests for APIs and external adapters.
testingApproach: Unit/contract coverage for deterministic APIs and adapters.
Cost events for AI Gateway and direct paid APIs.
mvpArchitecture: Keep starter state in .ai-starter as source of truth.
Add one feature plan before implementation.
Use manifests to connect features, tests, docs, costs, and evidence.

risks: The customer/problem/workaround chain is explicit but still needs real-world evidence.
Pricing assumptions still need payment or pilot evidence.
Distribution must be measured with a real conversion event.
Technical scope is manageable if verification remains strict.

validationExperiment.experiment: Run a landing-page/demo or concierge workflow for the named customer segment before broad build-out.
validationExperiment.successThreshold: At least 5 qualified conversations, 2 strong follow-ups, or 1 paid/LOI-style signal for the narrow MVP.

nextStep: Create the feature plan and implement only the MVP slice that proves willingness to pay.
